because of a phenomenon cause, called latent heat release.
Hurricanes often appear to intensify at night due to the increased release of latent heat during the cooler nighttime hours, as the upper atmosphere cools significantly without the sun's radiation, allowing more water vapor to condense into clouds and release significant amounts of heat energy that fuels the storm's intensity; this process is what drives a hurricane's "engine" and can lead to stronger winds and heavier rainfall overnight
